title: Nano Coders
tagline: Next-Generation EdTech & Coding Ecosystem for Youth
description: An award-winning EdTech platform and tech academy transforming coding into gamified, hands-on learning pathways.
problem: Traditional computer science education relies heavily on rote memorization and outdated syntaxes, leaving young students disengaged.
solution: Built an interactive, project-driven web platform combined with live mentorship cohorts. Students build real-world web apps and games from day one.
challenges: Scaling real-time code evaluation for hundreds of concurrent students.
results: Recognized by top national startup incubators
caseStudy: NanoCoders rapidly scaled into a nationwide movement, enabling students to learn modern technologies.

title: Kidspreneurship Bootcamp
tagline: Empowering Young Minds with Tech & Entrepreneurship
description: A comprehensive bootcamp hosted at The Mardan Lyceum to teach students about technology and entrepreneurship.
problem: Lack of practical tech and business education at an early age.
solution: Designed and led a series of interactive workshops ranging from 'Curiosity to Creation' to motivational tech talks.
challenges: Keeping young students engaged with complex technical concepts.
results: Fostered an entrepreneurial mindset in youth
caseStudy: The event concluded with high praise and a shield of appreciation for outstanding mentorship.

title: School Library Management
tagline: C# OOP Console Application for School Library
description: A functional C# console application that successfully manages school library books utilizing Object-Oriented Programming principles.
problem: Needed an efficient way to track and manage school library books in a text-based interface without relying on external databases.
solution: Built a Command-Line Interface (CLI) application with a main menu using C# Object-Oriented Programming (Classes). Implemented custom, reusable methods and robust error handling.
challenges: Ensuring correct usage of control structures and maintaining clean code execution.
caseStudy: The project successfully utilized control structures and loops to direct logic flow and maintain app persistence.

title: Inventory Management System
tagline: Robust CLI tool to track products and stock levels
description: A C# console-based inventory management system that tracks products, stock levels, and prices while ensuring robust input validation.
problem: Required a CLI-driven system to track products with strict functional requirements for adding, updating, viewing, and removing products without crashing on bad input.
solution: Developed a C# OOP application that uses loops and control structures for the main menu. Implemented AddProduct and UpdateStock methods with strict numeric validation.
features: View formatted list of tracked products
challenges: Validating inputs gracefully to ensure numeric values are provided for prices and stock so the program does not crash.
caseStudy: Demonstrated mastery of C# control structures, error handling, and object-oriented design.

title: Peshawar Todo List
tagline: Clean Todo List app built with Vanilla JS
description: A simple and clean Todo List app built while learning Full Stack Development with the Meta Professional Certificate on Coursera.
problem: Needed a practical project to apply HTML, CSS, and Vanilla JavaScript while managing states locally.
solution: Built a responsive web app using Vanilla JavaScript that saves tasks to localStorage to persist after refresh.
challenges: Handling state and filtering tasks efficiently without a framework.
caseStudy: Next steps include upgrading to a React.js Frontend and C# ASP.NET Core Web API Backend with a Database + Weather API for Peshawar. Built as part of the MicroSoft Full Stack Developer Certificate Project.
